title: polypodium hygroscopicum
intro: Polypodium hygroscopicum, also known as resurrection fern, is a type of epiphytic fern native to tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas. This plant is renowned for its unique ability to dry out completely, appearing dead, but reviving when exposed to water. The medicinal properties of Polypodium hygroscopicum include its use in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of the plant include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and boost the immune system, with herbal preparations typically involving infusions or decoctions of the plant's leaves.
